tool_args.label: MTA Board Meeting - January 21, 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Joint Committee and Board Meeting of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority, the New York City Transit Authority... Thursday, January 21, 2021, 10:00 a.m.

payload.description: Joint Committee and Board Meeting of the MTA and its agencies - January 21, 2021 meeting held at 2 Broadway, New York, NY

tool_args.label: MTA Board Meeting - February 18, 2021 (Action Items Meeting)
tool_args.raw_text: 2 Broadway 20th Floor Board Room New York, NY 10004 Thursday, 2/18/2021 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM ET

payload.description: MTA Board Action Items meeting at 2 Broadway 20th Floor Board Room, 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM ET

tool_args.label: TBTA 2020 Operating Surplus
tool_args.raw_text: The amount of surplus available for transfer to the MTA and NYCTA is $472,754,532.

tool_args.label: TBTA 2020 Investment Income Surplus
tool_args.raw_text: The amount of investment income that is surplus funds and available to transfer to MTA for fiscal year 2020 is $763,475.

tool_args.label: LIRR 2020 On-Time Performance
tool_args.raw_text: For 2020, our entire system collectively operated at 95.9%, exceeding our goal of 94%.

payload.scope: Long Island Rail Road entire system

tool_args.label: Metro-North 2020 On-Time Performance
tool_args.raw_text: on-time performance last year was a record high of 97.9%

tool_args.label: Metro-North 2020 Fleet Reliability
tool_args.raw_text: Overall fleet reliability in 2020 achieved a record 278,951 miles before a train broke down

tool_args.label: NYCT Subway 2020 On-Time Performance
tool_args.raw_text: NYCT has shown continued performance improvements in on time performance, which was 88.6% in 2020, the highest in recent history.

tool_args.label: Fare and Toll Vote Deferred
tool_args.raw_text: the MTA has decided to postpone a Board vote on fares today... With respect to tolls... the MTA will review the toll proposals in February.

payload.claim_text: MTA postponed Board vote on fare increases and will review toll proposals in February 2021; eliminating Staten Island and Queens resident discounts will not be considered.
payload.subject: MTA

tool_args.label: MTA Projected $11.3B Deficit
tool_args.raw_text: In December, MTA projected an aggregate $11.3 billion deficit for 2022-2024

payload.claim_text: MTA projected aggregate $11.3 billion deficit for 2022-2024 before cuts and expenses and other actions.
payload.subject: MTA

tool_args.label: CBDTP Bond Authorization up to $506M
tool_args.raw_text: issuance of TBTA CBDTP Second Subordinate Revenue Obligations in an amount not to exceed $506 million to fund costs related to the design, build, and installation of infrastructure, tolling systems and allowable implementation expenses necessary for the operation of the Central Business District Tolling Program

payload.claim_text: TBTA Board authorized issuance of CBDTP Second Subordinate Revenue Obligations up to $506 million to fund CBDTP infrastructure, tolling systems, and implementation expenses.
payload.subject: Central Business District Tolling Program

tool_args.label: Passenger Car Toll Increase - Tolls by Mail (Major Bridges/Tunnels)
tool_args.raw_text: An increase in the one-way passenger car Crossing Charge of $0.67 to $10.17 for customers using fare media other than NYCSC E-ZPass... at the Verrazzano-Narrows, Bronx-Whitestone, Throgs Neck and Robert F. Kennedy Bridges and the Queens Midtown and Hugh L. Carey Tunnels

payload.scope: Verrazzano-Narrows, Bronx-Whitestone, Throgs Neck, Robert F. Kennedy Bridges and Queens Midtown, Hugh L. Carey Tunnels

tool_args.label: E-ZPass Passenger Car Toll - Major Bridges/Tunnels
tool_args.raw_text: an increase in the passenger car charge for NYCSC E-ZPass customers of $0.43 to $6.55

payload.scope: Verrazzano-Narrows, Bronx-Whitestone, Throgs Neck, Robert F. Kennedy Bridges and Queens Midtown, Hugh L. Carey Tunnels

tool_args.label: Staten Island Resident E-ZPass Toll at Verrazzano-Narrows
tool_args.raw_text: A toll of $3.68 for registered Staten Island Residents ("SIR") using their NYCSC E-ZPass tag at the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge

tool_args.label: Rockaway Resident E-ZPass Toll at CBB/Rockaway Bridges
tool_args.raw_text: A toll of $1.60 for registered Rockaway/Broad Channel Residents using their NYCSC E-ZPass tag at the Rockaway Bridges

payload.scope: Cross Bay Veterans Memorial and Marine Parkway-Gil Hodges Memorial Bridges

tool_args.label: VNB SIR Rebate Amount
tool_args.raw_text: Change the rebate amount to $0.73 of an increased $3.68 toll amount for registered Staten Island Residents using their NYCSC E-ZPass tag at the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge

payload.description: Rebate of $0.73 toward $3.68 toll, resulting in $2.95 effective toll rate

tool_args.label: VNB Commercial Rebate Rate
tool_args.raw_text: Change the VNB Commercial Rebate rate to 15% for eligible trucks and other commercial vehicles on the VNB using their NYCSC E-ZPass tag.

payload.scope: Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge - eligible trucks and commercial vehicles

tool_args.label: CBB Rockaway Resident Rebate Amount
tool_args.raw_text: Increase the current rebate amount from $1.49 to $1.60 toward the new $1.60 toll amount for registered Rockaway/Broad Channel Residents using their NYCSC E-ZPass tag at the Cross Bay Veterans Memorial Bridge

payload.scope: Cross Bay Veterans Memorial Bridge - Rockaway/Broad Channel Residents
payload.description: Rebate matches toll amount resulting in $0.00 effective toll rate (toll fully rebated)

tool_args.label: SIR Effective Toll Rate After Rebate
tool_args.raw_text: resulting in a $0.20 increase in the effective toll rate (from the current amount of $2.75 to a new amount of $2.95)

payload.scope: Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge - Staten Island Residents after MTA SIR Rebate Program

tool_args.label: MRT-2 Escalator Payments to Dutchess, Orange, Rockland Counties
tool_args.raw_text: escalator payments totaling $8,334,346.53 to Dutchess, Orange and Rockland counties

payload.scope: MRT-2 Corporate Transportation Account payments to Dutchess, Orange and Rockland counties

tool_args.label: LIRR Corys Train Simulator Contract
tool_args.raw_text: $3,186,584 to Corys, Inc. to design, manufacture, test and deliver Train Simulator Systems for each of its M-7/M-9, Dual Mode (DM) Locomotive and C-3 fleets

payload.description: Competitively negotiated contract for M-7/M-9, DM Locomotive and C-3 Train Simulator Systems

tool_args.label: Alstom Five-Year Parts Pricing Agreement
tool_args.raw_text: a five-year pricing agreement contract for sole-source replacement parts including the propulsion controller, traction motor, and carbody to Alstom Transportation, Inc. in an estimated total amount of $7 million

payload.description: Five-year sole-source parts pricing agreement with Alstom for replacement parts for 2,427 subway cars

tool_args.label: C&D East Side Access Traction Power Modification
tool_args.raw_text: award of a modification (No. 50) to the East Side Access Traction Power Systems Package No. 4 contract (CS084) for furnishing and installing approximately 9,500 feet of 500 MCM stray current drainage cable for the C04 and C05 Traction Power Substations for the amount of $1,510,000

payload.scope: East Side Access Traction Power Systems Contract CS084

tool_args.label: C&D L.K. Comstock CBTC Modification (Ratification)
tool_args.raw_text: MTA C&D requests the Board ratify a retroactive modification for the removal and disposal of out-of-service equipment and associated hardware accessories on the Flushing Line from the Times Square Station in Manhattan to the Main Street Station in Queens, including the Corona Yard.

payload.scope: L.K. Comstock & Company Inc., Contract S-48006 - CBTC 8th Avenue Line & Flushing Line equipment removal

tool_args.label: Hartsdale Station Easement Acquisition
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North is investing in infrastructure improvements at the Hartsdale Station to support ADA accessibility and enhance pedestrian flow for its customers using the station. The Project includes 2 new elevators, mechanical rooms, expanded sidewalks and new curbing

payload.claim_text: MTA Metro-North authorized acquisition of permanent and temporary easements from Hartsdale Public Parking District in support of Hartsdale Station improvements including 2 new elevators, mechanical rooms, expanded sidewalks and new curbing for ADA accessibility.
payload.subject: MTA Metro-North Railroad
payload.location: Hartsdale, Town of Greenburgh, New York

location_normalized.raw_text: Hartsdale, Town of Greenburgh, New York

tool_args.label: MTA Small Business Development Program (SBDP)
tool_args.raw_text: the MTA's Small Business Development Program ("SBDP"), which celebrated its 10th anniversary earlier this week

payload.description: Program for minority- and women-owned businesses; 10th anniversary in January 2021

tool_args.label: SBDP 10-Year Contract Awards
tool_args.raw_text: Over the last decade, the program has awarded over 450 contracts amounting to nearly $500 million and created or maintained 11,000 jobs.

payload.scope: MTA Small Business Development Program over 10 years

tool_args.label: 2020 Capital Program Completions
tool_args.raw_text: the agency was able to complete $4.3B worth of the roughly $5.3B in completions originally planned at the outset of 2020

tool_args.label: TBTA 2020 Vehicle Crossing Volume vs 2019
tool_args.raw_text: paid vehicle crossings at TBTA facilities in 2020, as compared to 2019, were approximately 77%

payload.scope: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority facilities
payload.description: Approximately 77% of pre-pandemic (2019) paid vehicle crossing levels

tool_args.label: NYCDOT Bus Lane Additions 2020
tool_args.raw_text: NYCDOT's addition of more than 16 miles of bus lanes and bus ways in 2020 contributed to improved performance. These additions included the Jay Street bus way, the 149th street bus lane, the E.L. Grant Highway bus lane, 14th Street bus lane, the Merrick Blvd bus lane, the Highland bus lane and the installation of a bus way on Main street in Flushing, Queens.

payload.claim_text: NYCDOT added more than 16 miles of bus lanes and bus ways in 2020, including Jay Street bus way, 149th Street bus lane, E.L. Grant Highway bus lane, 14th Street bus lane, Merrick Blvd bus lane, Highland bus lane, and bus way on Main Street in Flushing, Queens.
payload.subject: NYCDOT bus lane additions

tool_args.label: NYCT Bus Additional Travel Time Reduction
tool_args.raw_text: on buses, additional travel time... decreased by 45 seconds on a 12-month average basis

tool_args.label: NYCT Bus Customer Journey Time Performance 2020
tool_args.raw_text: customer journey time performance increased by 5 percentage points to 77% over the last year

tool_args.label: NYCT Subway Customer Journey Time Performance 2020
tool_args.raw_text: customer journey time performance has also improved compared to the prior year, reaching nearly 86% in 2020

tool_args.label: TBTA 2020 Operating Revenues
tool_args.raw_text: OPERATING REVENUES $ 1,669,334,951

tool_args.label: TBTA 2020 Operating Expenses
tool_args.raw_text: OPERATING EXPENSES $ 442,212,121

tool_args.label: TBTA 2020 Debt Service on Bonds
tool_args.raw_text: DEBT SERVICE ON BONDS $ 675,801,044

tool_args.label: C&D Central Substation Renewal Modification
tool_args.raw_text: Ratified a retroactive modification... for the repair of existing duct banks and replacing positive feeder cables located between the Central Substation and the 51st Street Circuit Breaker House on the 6th Avenue Line for the amount of $3,580,000.

payload.scope: NYCT - Central Substation at West 53rd Street, Manhattan
payload.description: Retroactive modification for repair of duct banks and replacement of positive feeder cables between Central Substation and 51st Street Circuit Breaker House on 6th Avenue Line

tool_args.label: All-Agency Responsibility Guidelines Revision
tool_args.raw_text: The All-Agency Contractor Responsibility Guidelines, originally adopted in 1988, were last revised in 2010. The MTA Legal Department... recommends adoption of the revised Guidelines

payload.claim_text: MTA Board approved revised All-Agency Contractor Responsibility Guidelines including raising applicability threshold from $250,000 to $1 million, updating Significant Subcontract definition, and allowing agency Presidents to determine contractors responsible despite SAI for contracts up to $3 million.
payload.subject: MTA All-Agency Responsibility Guidelines

tool_args.label: Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP) - Bond Authorization
tool_args.raw_text: TBTA has entered into a Memorandum of Understanding with The City of New York and awarded a design, build, operate and maintain contract for the infrastructure and tolling collection system.

payload.description: TBTA Board authorized up to $506M in CBDTP Second Subordinate Revenue Obligations to fund design, build, and installation of infrastructure, tolling systems, and allowable implementation expenses.

tool_args.label: Maybrook Trailway Opening
tool_args.raw_text: she joined state and local officials to open the Maybrook Trailway, a twenty-three (23) mile segment of the greater Empire State Trail in Dutchess and Putnam Counties. The segment was built by Metro-North with $42 million in State funds

payload.description: Opening of the Maybrook Trailway, a 23-mile segment of the Empire State Trail in Dutchess and Putnam Counties, built by Metro-North with $42 million in State funds.

tool_args.label: Hartsdale Station ADA Improvements
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North is investing in infrastructure improvements at the Hartsdale Station to support ADA accessibility and enhance pedestrian flow for its customers using the station. The Project includes 2 new elevators, mechanical rooms, expanded sidewalks and new curbing

payload.description: 2 new elevators, mechanical rooms, expanded sidewalks, new curbing for ADA accessibility and enhanced pedestrian flow at Hartsdale Station
payload.location: Hartsdale, Town of Greenburgh, New York

location_normalized.raw_text: Hartsdale, Town of Greenburgh, New York

tool_args.label: Telecommunications and Utility Franchise License Policy
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Real Estate seeks to adopt a new policy covering such transactions. No current policy of MTA Real Estate expressly permits the same.

payload.claim_text: MTA Board approved new policy for awarding telecommunications and utility franchise licenses along MTA Agencies' rights of way, with terms up to 30 years including renewals, competitively offered through RFP process or non-exclusively without RFP.
payload.subject: MTA Real Estate policy

tool_args.label: MTA Automated Vehicle Location Monitoring System Contract
tool_args.raw_text: Approved a competitively negotiated miscellaneous service contract for LB Technology Inc. for implementation of Automated Vehicle Location Monitoring System (VLM) for all MTA Agencies for up to seven (7) years.

payload.claim_text: Board approved competitively negotiated miscellaneous service contract with LB Technology Inc. for implementation of Automated Vehicle Location Monitoring System (VLM) for all MTA Agencies for up to 7 years under Master Contract No. 15646.
payload.subject: MTA Finance Committee

tool_args.label: MRT-2 Dutchess County Escalator Payment
tool_args.raw_text: Dutchess Cty $2,263,755.16

payload.description: MRT-2 escalator payment from MTA to Dutchess County

tool_args.label: MRT-2 Orange County Escalator Payment
tool_args.raw_text: Orange Cty $2,689,068.65

tool_args.label: MRT-2 Rockland County Escalator Payment
tool_args.raw_text: Rockland Cty $3,381,522.72

tool_args.label: NYCT Subway Signal Timers Replaced - 900 mechanical to digital
tool_args.raw_text: replacement of more than 900 mechanical signal timers with digital units to improve reliability

payload.description: Replacement of more than 900 mechanical signal timers with digital units

tool_args.label: TBTA 2020 Collision Reduction
tool_args.raw_text: Collisions were down 50% during 2020

payload.description: Collisions were down 50% during 2020 compared to previous year

tool_args.label: TBTA 2020 Collisions with Injuries Reduction
tool_args.raw_text: collisions with injuries were down more than 37%

payload.description: Collisions with injuries down more than 37%

tool_args.label: LIRR December 2020 Monthly OTP
tool_args.raw_text: For the month of December, we exceeded our monthly OTP goal, hitting 95.6%.

tool_args.label: CBDTP Bond Authorization approved at February 2021 Board Meeting
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA Finance Department is seeking TBTA Board authorization... of the annexed supplemental resolutions... to provide for the issuance of TBTA CBDTP Second Subordinate Revenue Obligations in an amount not to exceed $506 million

tool_args.label: Hartsdale Station Improvements approved at February 2021 Board Meeting
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Real Estate requests authorization on behalf of Metro-North to acquire the easements from HPPD on the above terms and conditions.
